tasks.title: Audit and build complete action-to-event-to-game matrix
tasks.description: There are 88 Ensemble base actions in Prolog, 24 wired gameplay actions (14 core + 10 language learning), 11 physical hotspot actions, 45 quest objective types, 80+ GameEventBus event types, and 450+ unused Ensemble Prolog predicates. These systems are disconnected. Build a comprehensive matrix. (1) Create a spreadsheet/JSON file at shared/game-engine/action-matrix.ts that maps every base action name to: whether it has in-game execution code, which GameEventBus event it emits (or should emit), which quest objective type(s) it satisfies, whether it requires physical interaction (hotkey/radial menu), conversational interaction (detected from dialogue), or passive detection (proximity/observation). (2) Categorize each of the 88 base actions into: PHYSICAL (requires player to press a key near a target — buy_item, use_item, chop_tree, fish, etc.), CONVERSATIONAL (detected from dialogue — ask_for_directions, compliment_npc, introduce_self, etc.), OBSERVATIONAL (detected from watching/listening — eavesdrop, observe_activity, listen_and_repeat), AUTOMATIC (triggered by game state — sleep, rest, travel_to_location), INVENTORY (triggered from inventory UI — equip_item, consume, drop_item). (3) For each action, mark: EXISTS_IN_GAME (full implementation), PARTIAL (Prolog only, no game code), MISSING (not defined anywhere). (4) Identify the ~60 actions that exist as Prolog definitions but have zero game execution code. (5) Prioritize which missing actions are most important for gameplay (buy_item, sell_item, consume, craft_item, farm_*, chop_tree are high priority). Export this matrix for use by other task lists.

tasks.title: Fix Prolog action format errors across all 88 base actions
tasks.description: Every base action in the Actions editor shows the same Prolog errors: 'Missing required predicate: action/4', 'Content may have a clause missing a terminating period', 'Missing recommended predicate: can_perform/2', etc. This is a systemic format issue. (1) Read the current Prolog action content format (the 'action buy_item { ... }' curly-brace syntax shown in the editor) and compare it to what the Prolog metadata extractor (shared/prolog/prolog-metadata-extractor.ts) expects. (2) The action/4 predicate expects: action(Id, Name, Type, EnergyCost). The curly-brace format used by Ensemble doesn't emit this predicate. Either update the extractor to parse the Ensemble format, or create a transpiler that converts the Ensemble action syntax to standard Prolog predicates. (3) For each base action, generate the required predicates: action/4 (id, name, type, energyCost), can_perform/2 (actor conditions), action_effect/2 (effects list), action_duration/2 (time in game ticks), action_difficulty/2 (0.0-1.0). (4) This could be done by updating the ensemble-converter (shared/prolog/ensemble-converter.ts) to emit these predicates when converting Ensemble actions to Prolog. (5) Verify that after the fix, the Actions editor no longer shows errors for any base action. (6) Add a migration or batch update endpoint that re-converts all existing base actions.

tasks.title: Fix merchant system: business owners must be NPCs with merchant role and populated inventory
tasks.description: ROOT CAUSE DIAGNOSIS: Merchants are inaccessible due to THREE independent failures. (A) Business owners are not NPCs: in the LaLouisiane export, 4 of 5 businesses have owner IDs referencing characters who are NOT in the NPC list, so no merchant NPC exists in the game world. (B) Ineligible business types: 3 of 5 businesses have types (Theater, BarberShop, AutoRepair) ineligible for the settlement's population tier (hamlet/village), so no buildings are generated for them. (C) No 'merchant' role assignment: the export/IR generator never assigns role='merchant' to any NPC — all NPCs get 'questgiver' or 'civilian'. The resolveNPCRole() function in the spawner requires metadata.ownerId to match an actual NPC, but it never does. FIX PLAN: (1) In the IR generator (server/services/game-export/ir-generator.ts), when generating the NPC list, ensure every business owner character is included as an NPC. Currently the NPC list is a subset of characters — business owners must be in this subset. (2) In the NPC role assignment logic, when an NPC's character ID matches a business owner ID, assign role='merchant' instead of the default role. (3) In the settlement/building generation logic, ensure business types are selected from the eligible pool for the settlement's population tier — don't create businesses with types that won't get buildings. (4) Populate merchant NPC inventory via getMerchantInventory(): for FileDataSource, generate merchant inventory from base items filtered by businessType (bakery→bread/pastries, blacksmith→tools/weapons, shop→mixed goods). Currently FileDataSource.getMerchantInventory() uses a hardcoded helper that returns items but may not match the actual businessType. (5) In InteractionPromptSystem, when the player approaches an NPC with role='merchant', show 'Press E to Trade' and open BabylonShopPanel instead of the chat panel. (6) When a purchase completes, emit both item_purchased and action_executed events. (7) Test the full flow: approach merchant → see trade prompt → open shop → buy item → gold deducted → item in inventory.

tasks.title: Implement consume, use_item, equip_item, and drop_item inventory actions
tasks.description: Inventory-based actions (consume, use_item, equip_item, drop_item) have Prolog definitions but their in-game execution doesn't emit action events, preventing quest completion. (1) In BabylonInventory.ts, when the player uses an item (eat food → restore energy), emit action_executed with actionName='consume' and the item details. (2) When the player equips an item (weapon, armor), emit action_executed with actionName='equip_item'. (3) When the player drops an item, emit action_executed with actionName='drop_item' and spawn the item mesh in the world at the player's feet. (4) When the player uses a tool item (key, map, torch), emit action_executed with actionName='use_item'. (5) Each action_executed event should include: actionName, actorId (player), targetId (item), itemName, itemType, timestamp. (6) Wire these to QuestCompletionEngine so objectives like 'Use 3 health potions' or 'Equip the ceremonial sword' can track progress. (7) Add consume/use/equip/drop to the OBJECTIVE_TO_ACTION mapping in ActionManager.ts. (8) Show a Notification in the HUD when any action is executed: '[Action] You consumed a Croissant (+15 energy)'.

tasks.title: Fix container system: call outdoor spawning, populate containers with items, fix FileDataSource stubs
tasks.description: ROOT CAUSE DIAGNOSIS: Containers are inaccessible due to THREE independent failures. (A) Outdoor containers never spawn: ContainerSpawnSystem.ts has fully implemented generateOutdoorSpawnPoints() and spawnOutdoorContainers() methods, but BabylonGame.ts NEVER CALLS THEM. Interior containers work because registerInteriorContainers() IS called when entering buildings. (B) No container data in world exports: world_ir.json and buildings.json contain zero placed containers — the IR generator doesn't generate container placement data for the world. (C) FileDataSource stubs: FileDataSource.loadContainers() and loadContainersByLocation() return empty arrays — they're stub implementations that were never completed. The UI (BabylonContainerPanel), interaction detection (InteractionPromptSystem.registerContainer()), and opening handler (handleContainerInteraction()) all work perfectly — the problem is purely that no containers exist to interact with. FIX PLAN: (1) In BabylonGame.ts, after settlement buildings are spawned, call ContainerSpawnSystem.generateOutdoorSpawnPoints() to compute spawn locations near buildings, then call spawnOutdoorContainers() to create the actual container meshes (chests, barrels, crates). (2) In the IR generator, add container placement data to the WorldIR: for each building, generate 1-3 container placements with position, containerType, and initial items drawn from loot tables filtered by building businessType. (3) Export container data as containers.json (or embed in world_ir.json) so FileDataSource can load it. (4) Fix FileDataSource.loadContainers() to load from the exported container data file instead of returning empty arrays. (5) Fix FileDataSource.loadContainersByLocation() similarly. (6) Fix FileDataSource.transferContainerItem() to update the in-memory container state and persist to localStorage. (7) Populate containers with role-appropriate items: kitchen barrels → food, workshop chests → tools, bedroom dressers → clothing, locked chests → valuables/quest items. Each container should have 3-8 items. (8) Test the full flow: see container in world → approach → 'Press E to Open' prompt → open panel → see items → take item → item in inventory → container item removed.

tasks.title: Implement farm_plant, farm_water, farm_harvest, and chop_tree as physical actions
tasks.description: These actions exist as Prolog definitions but have no game code. Implement them as physical hotspot actions. (1) Add farm_plant, farm_water, farm_harvest to the physical action system (alongside existing fishing, mining, herbalism). Each needs: an action definition with energy cost, duration, and XP reward, a visual animation (use existing generic 'working' animation or farming animation if available from Quaternius), and item production (farm_harvest produces food items). (2) Add chop_tree as a physical action that can target tree meshes in the world. When the player approaches a tree and selects chop_tree from the radial menu, play the chopping animation, deduct energy, and after completion produce a 'Wood' item + XP. (3) Create farm hotspots at Farm buildings during world generation — place 3-4 farm plots with visual crop meshes near each Farm building. (4) Farm plots cycle through states: empty → planted → watered → harvestable. farm_plant transitions empty→planted, farm_water transitions planted→watered, farm_harvest transitions harvestable→empty+produces food. (5) Add these actions to the physical action radial menu. (6) Emit action_executed events for each so quests can track 'Plant 3 crops' or 'Harvest wheat' objectives.

tasks.title: Add natural world elements as interactable Base Items
tasks.description: Natural elements (trees, rocks, herbs, water features) are visual-only with no item representation. Make them interactable by connecting them to Base Items. (1) Create Base Items for tree types: Oak Wood, Pine Wood, Birch Wood, Willow Branch, Palm Frond, Maple Wood, Cypress Wood. These are produced by chop_tree when targeting the corresponding tree type. (2) Create Base Items for stone/mineral types: Granite, Limestone, Sandstone, Quartz, Flint. Produced by mine_rock when targeting the corresponding rock mesh. (3) Create Base Items for vegetation: Lavender, Rosemary, Thyme, Sage, Chamomile, Wild Mushroom, Dandelion, Clover. Produced by gather_herb when targeting herb meshes. (4) Add herb spawn points in forests and gardens during nature generation — place small herb meshes (colored sphere + stem, similar to flowers) with item metadata. (5) Add crystal/mineral spawn points near rocks and in caves. (6) When ProceduralNatureGenerator places trees, rocks, and herbs, tag each mesh with its Base Item type so the interaction system knows what item to produce when the player harvests it. (7) This enables quests like 'Gather 5 Rosemary from the garden' or 'Collect 3 Oak Wood for the carpenter'.

tasks.title: Build action quick menu with user-bindable hotkeys
tasks.description: Physical actions need a convenient input method beyond the radial menu. Build a quick action bar. (1) Create shared/game-engine/rendering/ActionQuickBar.ts — a Babylon.js GUI strip at the bottom of the screen showing 4 action slots (numbered 1-4 by default). (2) Each slot displays: action icon/emoji, action name, and keybind. Clicking a slot or pressing its key executes the action if conditions are met (near target, has energy, not on cooldown). (3) If the action requires a target (chop_tree needs a tree, buy_item needs a merchant), the game enters a 'target selection' mode where the next interaction (press E on a target) executes the bound action on that target. (4) Players can customize bindings: open the Action Quick Bar settings from the Game Menu to drag-and-drop actions into slots. Default bindings: 1=Examine, 2=Read Sign, 3=Take Photo, 4=Fish. (5) Some actions are already bound to dedicated keys and should NOT appear in the quick bar: E (interact/talk/enter), I (inventory), J (quest log), M (map), Escape (menu). Show these in a 'Controls' reference panel instead. (6) Store quick bar bindings in the save state. (7) Show available action slots with a subtle glow when the player is near a valid target for that action.

tasks.title: Build conversational action detection for NPC speech acts
tasks.description: Currently, ConversationalActionDetector only analyzes the PLAYER's messages (5 patterns: asked_about_topic, used_target_language, answered_question, requested_information, made_introduction). NPC responses are never analyzed for actions. Build NPC speech act detection. (1) After each NPC response in BabylonChatPanel, analyze the NPC's text to classify the speech act: gave_information (answered a question), refused_request, asked_question, offered_item, completed_transaction, taught_vocabulary (explained a word), gave_directions, told_story, assigned_quest. (2) For offline/exported games, use keyword+pattern matching (similar to ConversationalActionDetector but for NPC patterns: 'here are the directions', 'that will cost X gold', 'the word for X is Y'). (3) For online games with AI available, use a lightweight LLM classification call (similar to the existing metadata extraction endpoint). (4) When an NPC speech act is detected, emit an npc_action_detected event with: npcId, actionType, extractedData (e.g., directions text, vocabulary word, item name). (5) Wire npc_action_detected to QuestCompletionEngine: if a quest has an objective like 'Get directions from the baker', the npc_gave_directions event from that NPC should complete it. (6) When eavesdropping, apply the same NPC speech act analysis to both NPC speakers in the overheard conversation. (7) Show a Notification when an NPC action is detected: 'Marie taught you a new word: boulangerie (bakery)'.

tasks.title: Show action execution notifications in the HUD
tasks.description: When any action is executed (by the player, by an NPC in conversation, or by an NPC being observed), show a notification in the Notifications HUD panel. (1) In the GameEventBus, create a unified action_executed event type that captures: who performed the action (player or NPC name), what action was performed (action name from the base actions list), the target (item, NPC, location, object), the result (success/failure, items gained/lost, XP earned). (2) Subscribe to action_executed in BabylonGUIManager and display a toast notification: '[Player] fished at the river (+1 Salmon, +10 XP)' or '[Marie] sold you a Croissant (-3 gold)' or 'You overheard: [Pierre] told [Marie] about the missing writer'. (3) Color-code notifications by action category: green for successful actions, blue for learning actions, yellow for commerce, orange for combat. (4) Log action_executed events in the PlayerActionTracker for telemetry. (5) Add an 'Activity Log' section in the Game Menu that shows the last 50 action_executed events, filterable by category. (6) This makes all game actions visible and debuggable — if an action fires but doesn't complete a quest objective, the player (and developer) can see it in the log.

tasks.title: Populate the game world with sufficient items, containers, and merchants
tasks.description: Item-related actions (buy, sell, consume, equip, craft, use) require the world to be populated with items. Currently the world is sparse. (1) During world generation, populate containers in buildings with role-appropriate items from the 935 Base Items: kitchen containers get food items (bread, eggs, cheese, wine), workshop containers get tools (hammer, saw, chisel), bedroom dressers get clothing and personal items, shop containers get trade goods. (2) Each container should have 3-8 items drawn from weighted loot tables filtered by the building's business type. (3) Populate merchant NPC inventories with business-appropriate items: bakery merchants sell bread/pastries, blacksmiths sell tools/weapons, general stores sell mixed goods. Merchants should have 10-20 items for sale. (4) Place loose items in the world: books on shelves, tools on workbenches, food on tables, herbs in gardens. These can be picked up directly. (5) Ensure the IR generator exports all placed items and container contents so they appear in standalone exports. (6) Add item respawn for consumed/taken items — containers refill after 1 in-game day, merchant inventory restocks after 2 in-game days.

tasks.title: Build a simple crafting screen in the Game Menu
tasks.description: The CraftingSystem and RecipeCraftingSystem exist in shared/game-engine/logic/ but there's no crafting UI in the game menu. Build one. (1) Add a 'Crafting' tab to the Game Menu (GameMenuSystem.ts) alongside Inventory, Quests, Journal, etc. (2) Show a list of known recipes grouped by category: Food (cooking), Tools (smithing), Potions (alchemy), Materials (woodworking). (3) Each recipe shows: output item name and icon, required ingredients with quantities, whether the player has enough of each ingredient (green check or red X), crafting station requirement (if any: kitchen, forge, workbench). (4) 'Craft' button is enabled only when all ingredients are available and the player is near the required station (or has no station requirement). (5) Clicking 'Craft' deducts ingredients from inventory, adds the output item, awards XP, and emits item_crafted and action_executed events. (6) Generate 15-20 starter recipes during world generation based on available items and building types: settlements with bakeries get bread recipes, settlements with blacksmiths get tool recipes, etc. (7) Recipes can be discovered by finding recipe documents (text documents of type 'recipe') in the world.

tasks.title: Add animation previews to the Actions editor and bind animations to physical actions
tasks.description: Physical actions should have corresponding player animations, and the Insimul editor should show previews. (1) Catalog all available animations from the Quaternius character models and the animation registry (shared/game-engine/animation-registry.ts). Map each to a base action: idle, walk, run already mapped; add: fishing→fish, mining→mine, chopping→chop_tree, cooking→cook, farming→farm_plant/farm_harvest, reading→read_sign/read_text, praying→pray, sweeping→clean, crafting→craft_item, gathering→gather_herb, fighting→attack_enemy, waving→greet. (2) In the Actions editor (admin panel), add a preview panel on the right side that shows the animation for the selected action. Use a small 3D viewport (similar to ConfigPreviewScene) with the player model performing the action animation on loop. (3) For actions without a specific animation, show the 'working' generic animation. (4) In the game, when a physical action is executed, play the corresponding animation on the player mesh for the action's duration. The CharacterController should support playing action animations (some of this exists via AnimationAssetManager). (5) Store the animation mapping in the action's metadata so it's exported with the game.

tasks.title: Integrate action execution with the listen_and_repeat conversational flow
tasks.description: The listen_and_repeat action is defined in Prolog (requires near+in_conversation, effects: play_phrase, evaluate_pronunciation) and ListenAndRepeatAction.ts has scoring logic, but the full flow isn't integrated into conversations. Wire it up. (1) During NPC conversations, when the NPC says a phrase in the target language, add a 'Listen & Repeat' button below the NPC's message bubble. (2) Clicking the button triggers: play the NPC's phrase via TTS (Piper local or browser SpeechSynthesis), start recording the player's speech via the STT system (SpeechRecognitionService → Whisper → server fallback), compare the player's utterance against the NPC's phrase using scorePronunciation(). (3) Show the pronunciation score as a feedback popup: >=80% 'Excellent!', >=60% 'Good try!', <60% 'Try again'. (4) Emit utterance_evaluated with the score and action_executed with actionName='listen_and_repeat'. (5) Award XP based on score (4 XP base × score percentage). (6) Track listen_and_repeat completions for quest objectives of type 'pronunciation_check'. (7) The action should work both in active conversations and when eavesdropping (player repeats an overheard phrase).
